title: teaching nine agents to think like a colleague
summary: A developer decomposed a macOS AI assistant (VibeCat) into nine independent agents that mimic colleague behavior—vision detection, memory, mood sensing, celebration triggers, and decision-making—orchestrated through parallel and sequential execution waves.

fr.title: Enseigner neuf agents à penser comme un collègue

de.title: Neun Agenten trainieren, wie ein Kollege zu denken

title: QuickVideo – Batch Change Video Speed with Python and FFmpeg
summary: QuickVideo is a Windows desktop tool built with Python and FFmpeg that enables batch video speed adjustment with drag-and-drop support, real-time preview, and multi-format export while keeping audio perfectly synced.
content:
In this tutorial, we’ll build QuickVideo, a Windows desktop tool to batch adjust video speed with perfectly synced audio. You can drag & drop videos, preview changes, and export them in multiple formats.
You can download the EXE or clone the repository:
Windows EXE:
https://github.com/rogers-cyber/QuickVideo/releases
Source Code:
git clone https://github.com/rogers-cyber/QuickVideo.git
Project Setup
Install the required Python packages:
pip install ttkbootstrap tkinterdnd2

Ensure you have FFmpeg installed and its path configured:
FFMPEG_PATH = r"C:\ffmpeg\bin\ffmpeg.exe"
FFPLAY_PATH = r"C:\ffmpeg\bin\ffplay.exe"

Create the Main Window
We’ll use tkinterdnd2 for drag & drop and ttkbootstrap for a modern dark theme.
from tkinterdnd2 import TkinterDnD
import ttkbootstrap as tb

app = TkinterDnD.Tk()
app.title("QuickVideo – Change Video Speed v1.0.0")
app.geometry("1200x760")
tb.Style("darkly")

Define Application State
Keep track of videos, UI updates, and preview state:
video_list = []
ui_queue = Queue()
stop_flag = False
pause_flag = False
preview_process = None

Add File Selection UI
Create a file card with drag & drop support and buttons to add or clear videos:
files_card = tb.Labelframe(app, text="🎬 Video Files", padding=12)
files_card.pack(fill="x", padx=12, pady=6)

listbox = tk.Listbox(files_card, height=4)
listbox.pack(side="left", fill="x", expand=True)

scroll = tk.Scrollbar(files_card, command=listbox.yview)
scroll.pack(side="right", fill="y")
listbox.config(yscrollcommand=scroll.set)

Add buttons to add or clear videos:
tb.Button(files_card, text="➕ Add Files", bootstyle="success", command=add_videos).pack(side="left")
tb.Button(files_card, text="🧹 Clear", bootstyle="danger-outline", command=clear_videos).pack(side="left", padx=8)

Playback Speed and Output Settings
Add a slider for speed and preset buttons:
speed_var = tb.DoubleVar(value=1.0)
speed_lbl = tb.Label(app, text="1.00x", foreground="#22c55e")
speed_lbl.pack()

def update_speed(val):
    spee

title: I built 500+ free tools in 10 days — here's what actually happened
summary: A developer built 500+ free online utility tools (JSON formatters, encoders, validators) in 10 days using Python and FastAPI, discovering that SEO optimization and mobile compatibility required more work than actual code development.
content:
It started with a JSON formatter.
I was sitting at my desk at 11 PM, pasting some messy API response into one of those "free online JSON tools" — and the page had 14 ads, two newsletter popups, and a cookie banner bigger than the actual text area. The JSON formatted fine. But I felt gross using it.
So I closed the tab, opened VS Code, and started writing a JSON formatter. No plan. No Notion doc. Just annoyance and caffeine.
I finished the formatter in maybe 2 hours. And then I thought — okay, I already have this FastAPI route and this Jinja2 template... what if I just add a JSON validator too? Same skeleton, different logic.
That's how it starts. You build one thing and suddenly you're going "well I need a YAML converter anyway for work" and "Base64 encoder would take like 20 minutes" and before you know it you've got 50 of these things.
I hit 100 and genuinely lost count for a few days.
The setup is dead simple. Python route, Jinja2 template, vanilla JS doing the actual work in the browser. Your data never hits my server — not because I had some grand privacy philosophy, but because processing stuff client-side meant I didn't have to worry about server load or storage. Lazy architecture choices that accidentally became a feature.
Okay so Google basically pretends my site doesn't exist. 500+ pages, fresh domain, zero backlinks — I submitted my sitemap and... nothing. Like 7 pages indexed out of 500+. That's not a rounding error, that's Google telling you to go away.
Also some of my tools are rough. I'm not gonna pretend otherwise. The percentage calculator works but it looks like something from 2009. I went wide instead of deep and some pages show it.
The thing nobody tells you about building 500 pages? The META DESCRIPTIONS. And OG tags. And making sure nothing breaks on mobile. I spent more time on that nonsense than actually writing code. Building tools = fun. SEO housekeeping = slow death.
Oh and I'm on Render's free tier so the first person who visits after the

zh.title: 我在10天内构建了500多个免费工具——实际发生了什么
zh.summary: 一位开发者在10天内使用Python和FastAPI构建了500多个免费在线实用工具（JSON格式化器、编码器、验证器），发现SEO优化和移动兼容性需要比实际代码开发更多的工作。

fr.title: J'ai créé 500+ outils gratuits en 10 jours — voici ce qui s'est réellement passé
fr.summary: Un développeur a créé plus de 500 outils en ligne gratuits (formateurs JSON, encodeurs, validateurs) en 10 jours en utilisant Python et FastAPI, découvrant que l'optimisation SEO et la compatibilité mobile nécessitaient plus de travail que le développement du code lui-même.

de.title: Ich habe in 10 Tagen 500+ kostenlose Tools gebaut — Das ist tatsächlich passiert

es.title: Construí 500+ herramientas gratuitas en 10 días — esto es lo que realmente sucedió
es.summary: Un desarrollador construyó más de 500 herramientas en línea gratuitas (formateadores JSON, codificadores, validadores) en 10 días usando Python y FastAPI, descubriendo que la optimización SEO y la compatibilidad móvil requirieron más trabajo que el desarrollo del código real.

title: Ninja's Training
summary: The Ninja Training problem is a classic dynamic programming challenge where you schedule N days of activities (running, fighting, learning) to maximize merit points while avoiding consecutive repetition, solved through recursion, memoization, and tabulation.
content:
Here is your content formatted in a clean dev.to blog style (Markdown) so you can directly publish it on dev.to.
Dynamic Programming is one of the most important topics in DSA interviews and coding tests.
Ninja Training problem is a classic example that helps you understand several DP techniques step by step.
In this guide we will cover:
Problem intuition
Why greedy fails
Recursion approach
Memoization (Top-Down DP)
Tabulation (Bottom-Up DP)
Space optimization
Dry run example
Ninja is planning a training schedule for N days.
Each day he can perform one of three activities:



Activity
Meaning




0
Running


1
Fighting


2
Learning



You cannot perform the same activity on two consecutive days.
Maximize the total merit points.
points =
[1 2 5]
[3 1 1]
[3 3 3]

Optimal schedule:
Day 1 → Learning (5)
Day 2 → Running (3)
Day 3 → Fighting (3)

Total = 11

Consider this example:
10 50 1
5 100 11

Day1 → 50
Day2 → cannot choose 100 (same activity)
choose 11

Total = 61

Day1 → 10
Day2 → 100

Total = 110

Greedy fails because the best local choice does not guarantee global optimum.
Therefore we must explore all possibilities → Dynamic Programming.
We define a state:
dp[day][last]

Meaning:
Maximum points earned until "day"
if the last activity performed was "last"

Where:
last = 0 → Running
last = 1 → Fighting
last = 2 → Learning
last = 3 → No activity yet

Define a function:
solve(day, last)

Meaning:
Maximum points obtainable from day 0 → day if the previous activity was last.
solve(day,last) =
max(points[day][task] + solve(day-1,task))
for all task ≠ last

int solve(int day, int last, vector<vector<int>> &points)
{
    if(day == 0)
    {
        int maxi = 0;
        for(int task=0; task<3; task++)
        {
            if(task != last)
                maxi = max(maxi, points[0][task]);
        }
        return maxi;
    }

    int maxi = 0;

    for(int task=0; task<3; task++)
    {
        if(task != last)
        {
            int point = points[day][task] + solve(d

title: Running Anchor Tests on GitHub Actions Without Losing Your Mind
summary: Running Solana Anchor tests on GitHub Actions requires solving three critical issues: Agave 3.x's io_uring kernel dependency, GLIBC library mismatches, and container environment variable overrides that silently break cargo and rustup.
content:
Setting up anchor test on GitHub Actions sounds like a weekend task. It took me a full week of debugging to get it right. Here's
The context
I'm building an open source cross-chain bridge between Ethereum and Solana. The Solana side uses Anchor 0.31.1. At some point I
Simple enough, right?
Problem 1: Agave 3.x and io_uring
The first approach: install the Solana CLI inside the CI runner directly.
  - run: sh -c "$(curl -sSfL https://release.anza.xyz/stable/install)"

stable at the time of writing resolves to Agave 3.1.x. The install completes fine. Then anchor test starts the local validator and
  thread 'main' panicked at fs/src/dirs.rs:27:9:
  assertion failed: io_uring_supported()

Agave 3.x added a hard dependency on io_uring, a Linux kernel feature for async I/O. GitHub Actions runners run on kernels where
Fix: use Agave 2.x. v2.1.21 is the latest in the 2.1 series and works without io_uring.

  ENV SOLANA_VERSION=v2.1.21
  RUN sh -c "$(curl -sSfL https://release.anza.xyz/${SOLANA_VERSION}/install)"

Problem 2: GLIBC mismatch
Next issue: the anchor binary itself.
avm install 0.31.1 downloads a pre-built binary from GitHub releases. That binary was compiled on a system with GLIBC 2.38/2.39.
   /root/.avm/bin/anchor-0.31.1: /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libm.so.6:
     version `GLIBC_2.38' not found

Two fixes needed:
Switch the base image to Ubuntu 24.04 (ships with GLIBC 2.39)
Compile anchor from source instead of using avm. A binary compiled inside the container will always be compatible with the
container's GLIBC.
FROM ubuntu:24.04
  RUN cargo install \
    --git https://github.com/coral-xyz/anchor \
    --tag v0.31.1 anchor-cli --locked

This makes the build slower (adds ~5 min to the image build), but the image is built once and cached. Runtime CI stays fast.
Problem 3: Container environment quirks
When GitHub Actions runs a job inside a container, it overrides some environment variables. Two things break silently:
cargo can't find its toolchain:
error: rustup could

zh.summary: 在GitHub Actions上运行Solana Anchor测试需要解决三个关键问题：Agave 3.x的io_uring内核依赖、GLIBC库不匹配，以及悄悄破坏cargo和rustup的容器环境变量覆盖。

title: Finding the Right Reference Changed Everything for my Project
summary: A developer built a Wood Block Puzzle solver by discovering Block Blast Solver as a reference project, then adapted its proven engineering patterns for screenshot parsing and smart scoring algorithms.
content:
I got hooked on Wood Block Puzzle and eventually got frustrated enough to build a solver for it. Read the board from a screenshot, figure out where each piece fits best and show the optimal move. Sounded straightforward until I tried to plan the actual architecture.
Discovering a Working Reference
GitHub and dev forums looking for grid-based puzzle solvers. Most repos were either abandoned or built for Tetris, which has gravity mechanics that don't apply here. I needed something built for a static placement game that drop pieces on a board, clear rows and columns where no time pressure applies.
repo that was actively maintained. Block Blast shares the same fundamental mechanics as Wood Block Puzzle i.e grid board, shape placement, line clearing. The project even had a fully functional live version as Block Blast Solver with screenshot-based solving. The full pipeline was proven and running.

Three Things That Reshaped My Approach
Screenshot parsing is the real challenge. 
Smart scoring beats greedy scoring. 
Raw detection isn't enough. 
Adapting It
Having a working reference saved me weeks as the specific game barely matters but the engineering patterns underneath are universal.
Sometimes the smartest move in development is the same as in the game: look at the whole board before you place your first piece.

zh.title: 找到合适的参考项目改变了我的整个项目
zh.summary: 一位开发者通过发现Block Blast Solver作为参考项目来构建木块拼图求解器,然后采用其经过验证的工程模式来处理截图解析和智能评分算法。

fr.title: Trouver la Bonne Référence a Tout Changé pour mon Projet
fr.summary: Un développeur a construit un solveur de puzzle en blocs en découvrant Block Blast Solver comme projet de référence, puis a adapté ses modèles d'ingénierie éprouvés pour l'analyse de captures d'écran et les algorithmes de notation intelligents.

de.title: Die richtige Referenz zu finden hat alles für mein Projekt verändert
de.summary: Ein Entwickler erstellte einen Wood Block Puzzle Solver, indem er Block Blast Solver als Referenzprojekt entdeckte, und passte dann seine bewährten Engineeringmuster für Screenshot-Parsing und intelligente Scoring-Algorithmen an.

es.title: Encontrar la Referencia Correcta Lo Cambió Todo para mi Proyecto
es.summary: Un desarrollador construyó un solucionador de acertijo de bloques de madera descubriendo Block Blast Solver como proyecto de referencia, luego adaptó sus patrones de ingeniería probados para análisis de capturas de pantalla y algoritmos de puntuación inteligentes.

title: Vibe Coding Challenge — Day 12: Tattoo Generator
summary: Day 12 of a coding challenge created a tattoo generator tool (inkmachine.labdays.io), highlighting how modern development increasingly demands strong architecture and orchestration as implementation cycles compress from months to minutes.
content:
Create custom tattoos in dozens of different styles.
Announcement
inkmachine.labdays.io
Context
labdays.io) to learn about the process.
Notes from the 12th day of the Challenge
Now, it’s not enough to just have a great idea and turn it into a product. Even the most complex and monopolized software can be imitated in a short time. Considering that a framework like NextJS was rebuilt much faster — in just 1 week (by Cloudflare) — things are getting increasingly difficult.
Now, there are only minutes between ideas and implementation, not days, weeks, or months as before.
Architecture and orchestration are becoming increasingly important. Using powerful tools with the right algorithms allows you to turn their power into a usable product.
It’s beneficial for someone doing vibe coding to have knowledge of topics like machine learning, as it helps you better anticipate how AI models think and how they should be prompted.

zh.summary: 编码挑战第12天创建了一个纹身生成器工具(inkmachine.labdays.io),突出了现代开发如何越来越需要强大的架构和编排,因为实现周期从数个月压缩到数分钟。

fr.summary: Le jour 12 d'un défi de codage a créé un outil générateur de tatouage (inkmachine.labdays.io), soulignant comment le développement moderne exige de plus en plus une architecture et une orchestration fortes alors que les cycles de mise en œuvre se compriment de mois à minutes.

de.summary: Tag 12 einer Coding-Challenge erstellte ein Tattoo-Generator-Tool (inkmachine.labdays.io), das hervorhebt, wie moderne Entwicklung zunehmend starke Architektur und Orchestrierung erfordert, da sich die Implementierungszyklen von Monaten auf Minuten verkürzen.

title: How to Eliminate Logging Boilerplate in Java with CDI Interceptors
summary: CDI Interceptors in Java apply Aspect-Oriented Programming to eliminate logging boilerplate, automatically capturing method parameters, return values, execution time, and exceptions through a single annotation without manual code.
content:
Introduction


Logging is essential for monitoring, debugging, and performance analysis. 
However, manually adding log statements to every method creates several problems:
Code Clutter: Business logic becomes obscured by logging code
Inconsistency: Different developers log in different ways
Maintenance Burden: Changing log format requires updating every method
Incomplete Implementation: Easy to forget important details like execution time, exception handling, or parameter logging
CDI Interceptors solve these problems by applying the Aspect-Oriented Programming (AOP) principle, separating cross-cutting concerns from business logic.
With CDI interceptors, you can add logging to any method with a single annotation without requiring any manual coding for logging!
In this article, we’ll build a production-ready logging interceptor that automatically captures:
Method entry with parameter values
Method exit with return values
Execution time in milliseconds
Exception details with execution time
CDI (Contexts and Dependency Injection) Interceptors allow you to intercept method calls and execute code before and after the target method runs. They’re useful for cross-cutting concerns like:
Logging: Automatic method tracing
Security: Authorization checks
Transaction Management: Begin/commit transactions
Performance Monitoring: Method execution timing
Caching: Cache method results
Validation: Pre/post-condition checks
Client calls method
          │
          ▼
   ┌─────────────────┐
   │  @Logged        │ ◄──Annotation triggers interceptor
   │  PaymentService │
   └────────┬────────┘
            │
            ▼
   ┌─────────────────────────┐
   │  LoggingInterceptor     │ ◄── Intercepts the call
   │  @AroundInvoke          │
   │                         │
   │  1. Log entry + params  │
   │  2. Start timer         │
   │  3. context.proceed()   │ ◄── Execute actual method
   │  4. Log exit + duration │
   │  5. Return result       │
   └─────────────────────────┘
            │

zh.title: 如何使用CDI拦截器消除Java中的日志样板代码
zh.summary: Java中的CDI拦截器应用面向方面的编程来消除日志样板代码,通过单个注解自动捕获方法参数、返回值、执行时间和异常,无需手动编码。

de.title: Wie man Logging-Boilerplate in Java mit CDI-Interceptors eliminiert
de.summary: CDI-Interceptors in Java wenden Aspekt-orientierte Programmierung an, um Logging-Boilerplate zu eliminieren, und erfassen automatisch Methodenparameter, Rückgabewerte, Ausführungszeit und Ausnahmen durch eine einzelne Annotation ohne manuelle Codierung.

es.title: Cómo Eliminar el Código Boilerplate de Registro en Java con Interceptores CDI
es.summary: Los Interceptores CDI en Java aplican Programación Orientada a Aspectos para eliminar código boilerplate de registro, capturando automáticamente parámetros de método, valores de retorno, tiempo de ejecución y excepciones a través de una única anotación sin código manual.

title: Rust-like Error Handling in TypeScript

title: How AI and Custom Web Development Enhance Website Performance and UX
summary: AI-powered personalization on websites analyzes user data (location, preferences, browsing history) to deliver custom content and recommendations, improving engagement and conversion while meeting user expectations for sub-2-second load times.
content:
Today’s high-speed digital environment presents many new opportunities for businesses, but you have to be able to give your customers what they want quickly, easily, and personally in order to compete with other online businesses. The implementation of AI and custom web development has provided businesses with the tools necessary to turn their websites into the most effective, efficient, and customer-friendly sites. By combining these two technologies, businesses experience better website performance, improved UX, and greater efficiency overall.
When someone visits a website for the first time, their interaction with the site will define if they choose to remain on the website and continue to engage with it, or if they will leave immediately. Common issues that cause frustration and prevent visitors from continuing to use a website include slow load times, difficult navigation, and irrelevant content. Research shows that 47% of users will expect a webpage to load within 2 seconds or less, and 40% of them will abandon any site that takes longer than 3 second2.2 Chatbots and Virtual Assistantss to load.
AI technologies are revolutionizing the way websites interact with users. By analyzing large amounts of data, AI can improve various aspects of a website’s performance and design, offering a personalized experience that keeps users engaged.
AI algorithms analyze user data such as location, personal preferences, and browser history to create personalized content or product recommendations. Personalizing the experience provides greater relevance to each customer, creating increased engagement and higher chances of conversion.
For example, an advertising platform might recommend products based on previous purchases or browsing history, thus improving the user experience and providing additional revenue potential for the e-commerce website. Furthermore, personalizing the experience improves overall customer satisfaction and builds stronger ties to the brand through loyalty

zh.title: AI和自定义网络开发如何增强网站性能和用户体验
zh.summary: 网站上的AI驱动个性化分析用户数据（位置、偏好、浏览历史）来提供自定义内容和推荐，改善参与度和转化率，同时满足用户对亚2秒加载时间的期望。

fr.title: Comment l'IA et le développement Web personnalisé améliorent les performances et l'UX du site Web
fr.summary: La personnalisation alimentée par l'IA sur les sites Web analyse les données utilisateur (emplacement, préférences, historique de navigation) pour fournir du contenu et des recommandations personnalisés, améliorant l'engagement et la conversion tout en répondant aux attentes des utilisateurs en matière de temps de chargement inférieur à 2 secondes.

de.title: Wie KI und benutzerdefinierte Webentwicklung die Website-Leistung und UX verbessern
de.summary: KI-gestützte Personalisierung auf Websites analysiert Benutzerdaten (Standort, Vorlieben, Browsing-Verlauf), um benutzerdefinierte Inhalte und Empfehlungen bereitzustellen, das Engagement und die Konvertierung zu verbessern und gleichzeitig Benutzererwartungen für Ladezeiten unter 2 Sekunden zu erfüllen.

es.title: Cómo la IA y el desarrollo web personalizado mejoran el rendimiento y la UX del sitio web
es.summary: La personalización impulsada por IA en sitios web analiza datos de usuario (ubicación, preferencias, historial de navegación) para proporcionar contenido y recomendaciones personalizadas, mejorando el engagement y la conversión mientras se cumplen con las expectativas del usuario de tiempos de carga menores a 2 segundos.

title: Why Your Docking Station Fails to Detect an External Monitor: A Deep Dive into Multi-Display Architecture
summary: Docking station display detection failures stem from three core issues: USB-C bandwidth allocation conflicts across multiple monitors, EDID communication breakdowns, and protocol mismatches in DisplayPort Alt Mode lane reassignment.
content:
Why Your Docking Station Fails to Detect an External Monitor


A modern docking station is expected to transform a single USB-C or Thunderbolt port into an entire desktop I/O subsystem. Ethernet, storage, and multiple displays all converge on one cable. When everything works, the architecture feels invisible.
When it fails, however, the result is familiar to many IT administrators and workstation users: a perfectly functional monitor that simply refuses to appear in the operating system.
Many support tickets begin with the same phrase — a docking station not detecting monitor problem — but the root cause is rarely obvious. Multi-display docks operate on layered protocols such as USB tunneling, DisplayPort multiplexing, power negotiation, and EDID communication. A misalignment in any of these layers can prevent a monitor from initializing.
Understanding these failures requires looking beyond the connector and into the signal architecture itself.
Most display detection failures fall into three categories:
Bandwidth allocation limits
EDID communication failures
USB-C protocol mismatches
Although USB-C connectors look identical, the internal signal routing varies depending on host hardware.
When a device enters DisplayPort Alt Mode, the four high-speed lanes of the USB-C connector are dynamically reassigned. These lanes may be used entirely for video transmission or shared between USB data and DisplayPort.



Mode
Lane Allocation
Result




4-lane DP
Full DisplayPort bandwidth
Supports higher resolution multi-display


2-lane DP + USB 3.x
Shared bandwidth
Reduced video throughput



A full DisplayPort 1.4 HBR3 link provides up to 32.4 Gbps of bandwidth. Once this bandwidth is divided across multiple displays, limitations quickly appear.
Common examples include:
Dual 4K displays at 60 Hz
Ultrawide monitors (3440×1440)
High-refresh-rate panels
In these scenarios, the GPU may disable one output because the negotiated bandwidth cannot sustain both streams.
To the user, it a

zh.title: 为什么您的坞站无法检测外部监视器：多显示器架构的深入探讨
zh.summary: 坞站显示检测故障源于三个核心问题：跨多个显示器的USB-C带宽分配冲突、EDID通信故障和DisplayPort Alt模式车道重新分配中的协议不匹配。

fr.title: Pourquoi votre station d'accueil ne détecte pas un moniteur externe : une plongée profonde dans l'architecture multi-écran
fr.summary: Les défaillances de détection d'affichage de la station d'accueil proviennent de trois problèmes fondamentaux : les conflits d'allocation de bande passante USB-C sur plusieurs moniteurs, les défaillances de communication EDID et les incompatibilités de protocole dans la réattribution de voies du mode Alt DisplayPort.

de.title: Warum Ihre Dockingstation einen externen Monitor nicht erkennt: Ein tiefes Eintauchen in die Multi-Display-Architektur
de.summary: Ausfälle bei der Dockingstation-Anzeigeerkennung stammen aus drei Kernproblemen: USB-C-Bandbreitenzuweisungskonflikte auf mehreren Monitoren, Ausfälle der EDID-Kommunikation und Protokollabweichungen in der Neuzuweisung von DisplayPort Alt Mode-Spuren.

es.title: Por qué su estación de acoplamiento no detecta un monitor externo: un análisis profundo de la arquitectura de múltiples pantallas
es.summary: Las fallas de detección de pantalla de la estación de acoplamiento provienen de tres problemas centrales: conflictos de asignación de ancho de banda USB-C en múltiples monitores, averías en la comunicación EDID y desajustes de protocolo en la reasignación de carriles del modo Alt de DisplayPort.

title: Dispatch From the Other Side: Don't Let the Gap Grow
summary: Security guidance embedded in AI coding agents enables shift-left security by preventing issues before code is committed, allowing security teams to scale impact with near-zero remediation costs compared to production fixes.
content:
This is the fourth and final post of the Dispatch From the Other Side series.
From Scripts to Software
Designing For Leverage
Aligned Incentives
This week, one of the teams I work with released an internal API which provided an inventory of provisioned tenants for one of our platforms. I provided the OpenAPI spec to a coding agent. Within 10 minutes, I had a CLI and an agent skill so I could simply ask an LLM "Who owns that resource?" for operational or security response scenarios. Development teams are shipping code at that same pace. Can your security tooling keep up?
As I explored in my It Depends post, security practitioners can intervene earlier than ever before. Security expertise can be directly provided to coding agents, addressing issues before they're even committed to a codebase. This benefits both security teams and development teams by driving the cost to fix a security issue to near zero.
This is the promise shift left was always chasing. It lets security teams scale their impact. Rather than chasing an issue already in production, security guidance delivered directly to coding agents is high leverage by design.
In the Designing for Leverage post, we explored the example of using a minimal base image for containers instead of one with unnecessary packages that create extra work for development teams. A simple instruction to an LLM can enforce that standard at the point of code generation: 
When a base image is needed for Python, use the one available at company.registry.com/minimal-python:version
Security teams already analyze vulnerability and misconfiguration data to identify patterns. Those patterns often turn into documentation, tickets, or training. Now they can become agent instructions instead.
A couple of years ago, most conversations about LLMs focused on hallucinations. Today development teams are already shipping code with them. I saw something similar during the transition to cloud computing. Teams that weren’t curious enough about the shif

zh.title: 来自另一侧的消息：别让差距拉大
zh.summary: 嵌入AI编码代理中的安全指导能够实现左移安全，通过在代码提交前防止问题，允许安全团队以接近零的修复成本相比生产修复来扩大影响范围。

fr.title: Dépêche de l'autre côté : Ne laissez pas l'écart se creuser
fr.summary: Les conseils de sécurité intégrés aux agents de codage IA permettent une sécurité décalée vers la gauche en prévenant les problèmes avant la validation du code, permettant aux équipes de sécurité de maximiser l'impact avec des coûts de correction quasi nuls par rapport aux correctifs en production.

de.title: Meldung von der anderen Seite: Lassen Sie die Lücke nicht wachsen
de.summary: Sicherheitsleitlinien, die in KI-Codieragenten eingebettet sind, ermöglichen Shift-Left-Sicherheit, indem Probleme vor dem Commit des Codes verhindert werden, sodass Sicherheitsteams ihre Auswirkungen mit nahezu null Behebungskosten im Vergleich zu Produktionsfixes skalieren können.

es.title: Informe desde el otro lado: No dejes que la brecha crezca
es.summary: La guía de seguridad integrada en agentes de codificación de IA permite seguridad de desplazamiento a la izquierda al prevenir problemas antes de que se confirme el código, permitiendo que los equipos de seguridad amplíen el impacto con costos de remediación casi nulos en comparación con soluciones de producción.

title: Pi-hole vs Technitium DNS: Which Should You Use?
summary: Pi-hole excels at simple, focused ad blocking with a clean interface, while Technitium DNS provides a full-featured DNS server with zone hosting, clustering, and advanced capabilities—the choice depends on whether you need dedicated ad blocking or comprehensive DNS management.
content:
Quick Verdict


Pi-hole is better if you just want network-wide ad blocking with a clean dashboard. Technitium is better if you want a full DNS server that also blocks ads — it can host DNS zones, act as an authoritative nameserver, and cluster across multiple instances. For pure ad blocking, Pi-hole's larger community and simpler setup make it the easier choice.
Pi-hole is a DNS sinkhole focused on ad blocking. It proxies DNS queries, blocks ads and trackers using blocklists, and shows query statistics on a dashboard. Under the hood, it uses dnsmasq for DNS and lighttpd for the web UI.
Technitium DNS is a full-featured DNS server that happens to include ad blocking. It can serve as a recursive resolver (talking directly to root nameservers), an authoritative nameserver for your own zones, and a DNS proxy with blocking capabilities. It's built on .NET with a comprehensive web UI.

Technitium DNS

Yes

Yes (local DNS)
Yes (full zone management)



Pi-hole is straightforward — one container, web UI accessible immediately, guided setup through the interface. You're blocking ads within 5 minutes. Adding recursive DNS requires a second container (Unbound).
Technitium is also a single container, but the web UI exposes far more configuration options. The initial setu

title: Beyond Native: A Strategic Guide to Cross-Platform Framework Selection
summary: This strategic framework helps technical leaders select cross-platform development solutions by analyzing architectural patterns, performance implications, and organizational impact—moving beyond marketing claims to inform decisions affecting team structure and long-term maintainability.
content:
Beyond Native: A Strategic Guide to Cross-Platform Framework Selection



  
  
  Abstract


The modern digital landscape demands applications that reach users across iOS, Android, web, and desktop platforms. While native development offers peak performance and platform integration, the resource investment is often prohibitive. Cross-platform frameworks have evolved from simple web wrappers to sophisticated toolkits capable of delivering near-native experiences. This article provides technical leaders and senior developers with a comprehensive, actionable framework for selecting the right cross-platform solution. We'll examine architectural patterns, performance characteristics, and real-world implementation strategies, moving beyond marketing claims to practical engineering considerations.
The proliferation of devices and platforms has created a fundamental challenge for development teams: how to deliver consistent, high-quality user experiences across multiple operating systems without multiplying engineering effort. According to recent surveys, over 60% of development teams now use some form of cross-platform technology, driven by the need for faster time-to-market, reduced maintenance overhead, and unified codebases.
However, the "write once, run anywhere" promise often comes with significant trade-offs. The selection of a cross-platform framework isn't merely a technical decision—it's a strategic one that impacts team structure, development velocity, long-term maintainability, and ultimately, product success. This guide moves beyond superficial comparisons to examine the architectural foundations, performance implications, and organizational considerations that should inform your framework selection.
Cross-platform frameworks generally fall into three architectural categories, each with distinct characteristics:
Representative Framework: Apache Cordova/Ionic
Architecture: Web technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) wrapped in a native container with bridge APIs to

zh.title: 超越原生：跨平台框架选择战略指南
zh.summary: 这个战略框架通过分析架构模式、性能含义和组织影响，帮助技术领导者选择跨平台开发解决方案——超越营销宣传，为影响团队结构和长期可维护性的决策提供信息。

de.summary: Dieses strategische Framework hilft technischen Führungskräften, Cross-Platform-Entwicklungslösungen auszuwählen, indem es architektonische Muster, Leistungsauswirkungen und organisatorische Auswirkungen analysiert—über Marketing-Versprechungen hinausgehend, um Entscheidungen zu treffen, die die Teamstruktur und langfristige Wartbarkeit beeinflussen.

es.title: Más allá de Native: Una guía estratégica para la selección de marcos multiplataforma
es.summary: Este marco estratégico ayuda a los líderes técnicos a seleccionar soluciones de desarrollo multiplataforma analizando patrones arquitectónicos, implicaciones de rendimiento e impacto organizacional—yendo más allá de las afirmaciones de marketing para informar decisiones que afecten la estructura del equipo y la mantenibilidad a largo plazo.

title: FAQ: Stolen Valid Credentials — How Attackers Move Through Your Infrastructure Undetected
summary: Stolen valid credentials enable attackers to move through infrastructure as legitimate users without detection, representing 380% growth in identity-centric attacks—credentials from GitHub leaks, phishing, or supply chain compromise provide immediate access unlike breached passwords requiring cracking.
content:
TL;DR


380% growth in identity-centric attacks. Attackers steal valid credentials and move through your infrastructure as legitimate users. Your endpoint protection sees nothing because it's legitimate access.
A: API keys, OAuth tokens, session cookies, or database passwords that attackers obtain and use to authenticate to your systems as if they were authorized users.
Examples:
AWS access key (attacker logs in as that user)
GitHub personal access token (attacker clones private repos)
Okta session cookie (attacker logs into your SaaS admin portal)
Database password (attacker queries your database)
Stripe API key (attacker queries customer payment data)
Key difference from malware: 
Malware = attacker plants malicious code
Stolen credentials = attacker uses legitimate access
A: Two critical differences:
Attacker steals password hash from database
Attacker cracks hash (slow, requires dictionary/brute force)
Attacker tries password on multiple services
Attacker needs MFA bypass if enabled
Attacker obtains valid token/key directly (GitHub scan, phishing, supply chain)
Token is ready-to-use (no cracking needed)
Attacker already authenticated
MFA often not enabled on API keys/tokens
Attacker can use immediately
Result: Stolen credentials = immediate access. Breached passwords = slower, harder exploitation.
A: Six primary vectors:
GitHub Leaks (2M+/month)





# Attacker finds leaked AWS credentials in public repo
$ git clone https://github.com/your-company/project
$ git log -p | grep -i AWS_ACCESS
# Finds credentials in commit history

Phishing Attacks (OAuth token theft)





Attacker sends email: "Click here to re-verify your Slack account"
Link goes to attacker-controlled site that mimics Slack login
Victim enters credentials
Attacker intercepts OAuth token
Token is valid, MFA was bypassed, attacker has admin access

Supply Chain Compromise (Third-party code execution)





# Attacker compromises npm package
# Package logs all environment variables on install
# 100M+

zh.title: 常见问题：被盗的有效凭证——攻击者如何在不被发现的情况下在您的基础设施中移动
zh.summary: 被盗的有效凭证使攻击者能够以合法用户身份在基础设施中移动而不被发现，代表身份中心攻击增长380%——来自GitHub泄漏、网络钓鱼或供应链妥协的凭证提供即时访问，不同于需要破解的被破坏密码。

fr.title: FAQ: Identifiants valides volés — Comment les attaquants se déplacent dans votre infrastructure sans être détectés
fr.summary: Les identifiants valides volés permettent aux attaquants de se déplacer dans l'infrastructure en tant qu'utilisateurs légitimes sans être détectés, représentant une croissance de 380% des attaques centrées sur l'identité—les identifiants provenant de fuites GitHub, d'hameçonnage ou de compromis de la chaîne d'approvisionnement offrent un accès immédiat contrairement aux mots de passe compromis nécessitant un craquage.

de.title: Häufig gestellte Fragen: Gestohlene gültige Anmeldedaten — Wie Angreifer unerkannt durch Ihre Infrastruktur eindringen
de.summary: Gestohlene gültige Anmeldedaten ermöglichen es Angreifern, sich als legitime Benutzer durch die Infrastruktur zu bewegen, ohne erkannt zu werden, was einen Anstieg von 380% bei identitätsgesteuerten Angriffen darstellt—Anmeldedaten aus GitHub-Lecks, Phishing oder Lieferkettenkaptur bieten sofortigen Zugriff im Gegensatz zu gebrechenen Passwörtern, die geknackt werden müssen.

es.title: Preguntas frecuentes: Credenciales válidas robadas — Cómo los atacantes se mueven a través de su infraestructura sin ser detectados
es.summary: Las credenciales válidas robadas permiten a los atacantes moverse a través de la infraestructura como usuarios legítimos sin ser detectados, representando un crecimiento del 380% en ataques centrados en la identidad—las credenciales de filtraciones de GitHub, phishing o compromisos de la cadena de suministro proporcionan acceso inmediato a diferencia de las contraseñas comprometidas que requieren descifrar.

title: Why Your Team Bought GitHub Copilot (And Why Nobody's Using It)
summary: Most GitHub Copilot deployments fail due to workflow integration issues rather than tool limitations; success requires identifying specific workflow improvements and positioning AI as an amplifier of existing processes, not a code replacement.
content:
You rolled out Copilot. You paid for the licenses. You sent the announcement email.
Six weeks later, usage metrics look like a ski slope — steep drop-off after week one.
If this sounds familiar, you're not alone. Adoption of AI coding tools is failing at most companies, and the reasons have almost nothing to do with the tool itself.
Here's what's actually happening — and what actually works.
When developers get access to a new AI tool, they try it for the obvious things first: "Write me a function that does X." Sometimes it works great. Sometimes it halts output, generates something weird, they hit backspace, and go back to their normal workflow.
The tool didn't fail. The workflow integration did.
AI tools like Copilot and Claude Code aren't drop-in replacements for anything. They're amplifiers — but only if you already have a workflow to amplify. Most developers don't get training on how to integrate the tool into their daily work. They get a license and a Confluence page.
Mistake #1: Mandating the tool without changing the workflow
If you tell your team "use Copilot for all new features" but your PR review process, your definition of done, and your velocity metrics stay the same — you're adding friction, not removing it. Developers will use the tool to generate boilerplate, then spend twice as long understanding what it wrote. They'll stop.
The fix: Pick one part of the workflow to improve first. Sprint retros? Copilot summarizes discussion notes. Code review comments? Copilot explains context. Pick one, build a habit, expand.
Mistake #2: Treating it as a junior developer who writes code
The mental model that AI "writes code for you" sets teams up for disappointment. It does write code — but that's not the highest-value use. The highest-value uses are:
Explaining unfamiliar codebases ("What does this module do?")
Generating test cases for existing code
Writing documentation that actually gets written
Translating between languages (Python to TypeScript, etc.)
Summa

zh.title: 为什么您的团队购买了GitHub Copilot（以及为什么没人在使用它）
zh.summary: 大多数GitHub Copilot部署失败是由于工作流集成问题而不是工具限制；成功需要识别具体的工作流改进并将AI定位为现有流程的放大器，而不是代码替代品。

fr.title: Pourquoi votre équipe a acheté GitHub Copilot (et pourquoi personne ne l'utilise)
fr.summary: La plupart des déploiements de GitHub Copilot échouent en raison de problèmes d'intégration de flux de travail plutôt que de limitations d'outils; le succès nécessite d'identifier des améliorations de flux de travail spécifiques et de positionner l'IA comme un amplificateur des processus existants, et non comme un remplacement de code.

de.title: Warum Ihr Team GitHub Copilot gekauft hat (und warum niemand es nutzt)
de.summary: Die meisten GitHub Copilot-Bereitstellungen scheitern aufgrund von Workflow-Integrationsproblemen und nicht aufgrund von Werkzeuglimitierungen; Der Erfolg erfordert die Identifizierung spezifischer Workflow-Verbesserungen und die Positionierung von KI als Verstärker bestehender Prozesse, nicht als Code-Ersatz.

es.title: Por qué su equipo compró GitHub Copilot (y por qué nadie lo está usando)
es.summary: La mayoría de los despliegues de GitHub Copilot fallan debido a problemas de integración del flujo de trabajo en lugar de limitaciones de herramientas; el éxito requiere identificar mejoras específicas del flujo de trabajo y posicionar la IA como un amplificador de procesos existentes, no como un reemplazo de código.

title: I Made My AI Write Its Own Weekly Report. The Numbers Surprised Me.
summary: Running Claude Code autonomously for seven weeks produced 73 hours of AI work per week across multiple projects with 145,000+ lines of code added, demonstrating the scale of parallel AI development while revealing the challenge of tracking what autonomous systems accomplish.
content:
I've been running Claude Code autonomously for about 7 weeks. It writes code, builds tools, publishes things to npm, creates articles, runs tests — all without me watching.
I had no idea how much it was actually doing.
So I built a tool to find out.
npx cc-weekly-report

Output for the past 7 days:
# AI Weekly Report: 2026-02-22 – 2026-02-28

## Summary
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Days active | **7 / 7** |
| Total sessions | **530** |
| Total AI time | **73h 15m** |
| Lines added | **+145,793** |
| Lines removed | **-4,272** |
| Files touched | **1788** |
| CC tool calls | **4235** |

73 hours. In 7 days. While I was sleeping, working, doing other things.
## Top Projects
| Project | Sessions | Time | Lines added |
|---------|----------|------|-------------|
| project-a | 371 | 48.4h | +101,143 |
| cc-loop | 32 | 1.7h | +15,224 |
| namakusa | 78 | 21.2h | +15,968 |

The top project isn't one of the tools I talk about on this blog. It's a scheduling optimization system for a client. 48 hours in one week. The AI was deep in integer programming, constraint solvers, and scheduling algorithms while I was doing other things.
That's the thing nobody tells you about autonomous AI development: you lose track of what your AI is doing.
Not because it's hiding anything. Because it's working on 6 things at once and the output is too fast to follow.
My proof-log system (automated session records in ~/ops/proof-log/) already tracked everything:
### 2026-02-28 23:22-01:11 JST — セッション終了

- どこで: project-a
- 何を: 9ファイル変更 (+566/-97)
  - optimizer.py (+72/-0)
  - test_optimizer.py (+27/-0)
- どうやって: Edit: 17回, Write: 4回

But reading 530 of these entries manually isn't possible. The weekly report collapses them into something human-readable.
Zero dependencies. Reads ~/ops/proof-log/YYYY-MM-DD.md files, parses session entries, aggregates by project and day.
# Last 7 days (default)
npx cc-weekly-report

# Last 14 days
npx cc-weekly-report --days 14

# Save to file
npx cc-weekly-re

zh.title: 我让我的AI写自己的周报。数字让我惊讶。
zh.summary: 连续七周运行Claude Code自主生成了每周73小时的AI工作量，跨越多个项目添加了145,000多行代码，展示了并行AI开发的规模，同时揭示了跟踪自主系统完成工作的难度。

fr.title: J'ai fait écrire à mon IA son propre rapport hebdomadaire. Les chiffres m'ont surpris.
fr.summary: L'exécution autonome de Claude Code pendant sept semaines a produit 73 heures de travail IA par semaine sur plusieurs projets avec plus de 145 000 lignes de code ajoutées, démontrant l'ampleur du développement IA parallèle tout en révélant le défi de suivre ce que les systèmes autonomes accomplissent.

de.title: Ich ließ meine KI ihren eigenen Wochenbericht schreiben. Die Zahlen überraschten mich.
de.summary: Die siebenwöchige autonome Ausführung von Claude Code erzeugte 73 Stunden KI-Arbeit pro Woche über mehrere Projekte mit über 145.000 hinzugefügten Codezeilen und demonstrierte das Ausmaß der parallelen KI-Entwicklung, während es die Herausforderung der Verfolgung dessen aufzeigte, was autonome Systeme erreichen.

es.title: Hice que mi IA escribiera su propio informe semanal. Los números me sorprendieron.
es.summary: La ejecución autónoma de Claude Code durante siete semanas produjo 73 horas de trabajo de IA por semana en múltiples proyectos con más de 145.000 líneas de código agregadas, demostrando la escala del desarrollo de IA paralelo mientras revelaba el desafío de rastrear lo que los sistemas autónomos logran.
